reviews.review_description: It really does.
This is a small and deeply touching addition to  America's history that is still relevant today, unfortunately. We need more and more, and more of this. High quality work to say the least.

It's amazing how many white people are triggered by a game that teaches important history and basically say to forget about it and get over it in a much more passive-agressive way. There's a saying about those that forget history... Seems these days privileged people are offended by everything that puts anything but a positive spotlight on themselves, quick to call anything else political pandering. I'm wondering how many of them downloaded it just to leave a bad review without even playing it?

reviews.review_title: Bruh I thout this was a game
reviews.review_description: Dude are you kidding me I thout this was a game idk why I barely watched the preview I’m so mad

People play VR specifically to get AWAY from politics!

We do not need it or want it in our apps! 

-5 Stars ⭐️ ⭐️ ⭐️ ⭐️ ⭐️

There is a not a lot of character customization, for example it forces me to be black, this game is also very racist making me sit in the back of the bus
Very racist game, don’t play it

reviews.review_title: TRY TO NOT BE FRAGILE AND TRIGGERED
reviews.review_description: Of course there are a couple of fragile yt guys who are offended and triggered that there’s a VR experience that provides a view into the experiences of people unlike them. Ignore their trash and undeserved, unearned privilege and educate yourselves.

The bonus of having a virtual reality headset is the ability to watch immersive documentaries such as Travelling While Black. Being able to sit with those storytellers allows you to be able to feel their words more deeply. Highly recommend watching this.

This was absolutely amazing i e watched it twice. I felt as if I was sitting right there as if I could reach out and touch everyone and everything. 

The conversation with Tamir Rices mum was so well done, a few times I felt like she was looking right at me. I shed some tears when she was explaining the aftermath of her son  being murdered.
Brought me to tears. Not a game, but an experience.

I get the reason and also it states for educational purposes. To show how far we have come. Yet it clearly states with in the film. Nothing has changed. 
I don’t wish to express in colour, but from a point of being a human being. 
I hope for the day this will be the only term used to express.
This was an amazing experience. I strongly recommend.
An important story and so great to sit next to the storytellers. Well done!

Depending on a person situation, I always feel reminders of good or bad times as a person of color is good.  This is my personal opinion for myself.  Reminders are for motivation,  to basically keep pushing myself harder to achieve my goals.
Thank you for this.

I read reviews where people said this is the past let it go. Obviously they didn't watch it, because Tamir Rice story came up and that was in Nov 2014. I like this, very informative just wish it had more content and cases. Will show my family and friends. Also the graphics was on point
great cinematography, casting, and immersive scenes. very, very well done!

Amazing perspective on recent history in the lives of African Americans, and even brings it forth to present day. Some people seem to have forgotten that segregation wasn’t even that long ago, we have people alive who have lived through it. Having a documentary on quest was so interesting, it definitely feels like you’re there and are a part of the conversation. I cried multiple times throughout and was engaged the entire time. This is not a liberal documentary, race and history isn’t partisan, and you’re a fool if you think that to be so.
This really gives you a different perspective. I really enjoyed it. I’d love to see more docudramas made like this.
With an excellent message. I hope this can help educate even just one more person to the travesties of what takes place in the US.
